Magazine

Wine Taverns in Trier: The Best Spots for Mosel Wine

1 October 2024

Trier is the oldest city in Germany and is known not only for its Roman heritage but also for a lively wine culture. The city sits in the middle of the Mosel-Saar-Ruwer wine region, and you can taste it in the many wine taverns between the city center and Olewig (a wine village within Trier). If you want to round off a day spent between the Porta Nigra (the Black Gate) and the Hauptmarkt (the main market square), you will find plenty of inviting addresses here for Riesling, regional cuisine and easygoing evenings.

Why Trier is a wine city

Trier sits at the heart of the Mosel wine region, one of the most tradition-rich Riesling areas in the world. The Romans were already cultivating vines on the steep slate slopes here, and that tradition still shapes the city today. Around the Trier district of Olewig, the vineyards run almost up to the houses themselves.

Trier's wine taverns range from historic press houses to modern wine and tapas bars. What they share is a focus on regional bottles from the Mosel, Saar and Ruwer rivers. If you want to dig deeper, you can also explore the area along the Weinkulturpfad in Olewig (the Olewig wine culture trail).

The best wine taverns in the city center

In Trier's city center, several addresses are clustered within easy walking distance, making them ideal for a stroll through the old town. Das Weinhaus on Brückenstraße carries a wide selection of white and red wines from the Mosel, Ruwer and Saar, rounded out by sparkling wines. Ju Zwölf on Palaststraße pairs an international wine list with a curated gin selection, small bites and Flammkuchen (Alsatian-style flatbread).

If you prefer a Mediterranean style, Tapavino is a solid choice for Spanish cooking and tapas. Robertinos Vinothek und Tapasbar on Karl-Marx-Straße also leans into Spanish tapas and matching wines. Kost-Bar stands out for its modern interior, Mosel Riesling and international wines, served alongside cheese platters and tapas.

  • Das Weinhaus, Brückenstraße 7: broad selection of Mosel, Saar and Ruwer wines
  • Ju Zwölf, Palaststraße 12: wine and gin bar with Flammkuchen
  • Tapavino: Spanish cuisine and tapas in a cozy setting
  • Kost-Bar: Mosel Riesling, international wines, cheese and tapas
  • Robertinos Vinothek & Tapasbar, Karl-Marx-Straße 31: Spanish tapas and wines

Wine taverns in Olewig and on the city outskirts

Around the vineyards in Olewig you will find addresses with a particularly close connection to the wine region. Beckers Weinhaus on Olewiger Straße serves hearty regional home cooking in a relaxed atmosphere. Weinwirtschaft Friedrich-Wilhelm occupies a historic press house, pours wines from the Bischöfliche Weingüter (Episcopal Wine Estates) and pairs them with Mediterranean dishes.

Gutsweinstuben E. von Nell is located in the Tiergarten area and is known for solid traditional German cooking, while Deutschherrenhof offers regional Mosel wines and homemade dishes. Öchsle calls itself a wine and fish house, combining wine tastings with its own fresh fish counter. An evening in Olewig pairs nicely with a walk through the surrounding vineyards.

  • Beckers Weinhaus, Olewiger Straße 206: regional home cooking, local specialties
  • Weinwirtschaft Friedrich-Wilhelm: historic press house, wines from the Bischöfliche Weingüter
  • Gutsweinstuben E. von Nell, Im Tiergarten 12: traditional German cuisine
  • Deutschherrenhof: regional Mosel wines and homemade dishes
  • Öchsle: wine and fish house with tastings and a fresh fish counter

Tips for the perfect wine evening in Trier

Most wine taverns open only in the late afternoon or evening, and some close one day at the start of the week. Reservations are especially worthwhile on weekends and during the wine festivals. Current opening hours and prices should be checked directly with each venue before your visit, as these can shift with the season.

If you want to learn more about the region, combine your visit with a wine tasting or a guided tour. Our tips on wine festivals and Mosel wine tours are a natural fit. That way, a single glass of Riesling can easily turn into a full day of pleasure along the Moselle.

Frequently asked questions

Which wine tavern in Trier is best for Mosel Riesling?

For classic Mosel Riesling, Das Weinhaus on Brückenstraße and Kost-Bar are both excellent choices. Each carries a broad selection of regional wines from the Mosel, Saar and Ruwer.

Are there wine taverns in Trier that serve tapas?

Yes. Tapavino and Robertinos Vinothek focus on Spanish tapas, and Kost-Bar also serves cheese platters and tapas to go with the wine.

Where can I find wine taverns close to the vineyards?

In the Olewig district, Beckers Weinhaus and Weinwirtschaft Friedrich-Wilhelm sit right at the edge of the vineyards. The Olewig wine culture trail (Weinkulturpfad) pairs perfectly with a visit.

Do I need to make a reservation at Trier's wine taverns?

On weekends and during the wine festivals, a reservation is recommended. Many places also open only in the afternoon or evening and have one closing day per week.
